problem_addressed: Reward models in RLHF overlook epistemic uncertainty from limited human feedback, leading to reward hacking and alignment issues.
core_claim: RewardUQ provides a unified framework that systematically evaluates uncertainty quantification methods, revealing model size and initialization as the most impactful factors.
key_contributions: Introduces RewardUQ framework for systematic UQ evaluation
Compares four UQ architectures (ENS-MLP, ENS-LoRA, MCD-DPO, BAY-LIN) on accuracy/calibration metrics
Proposes new ranking strategy combining accuracy and calibration
Releases open-source Python package for UQ evaluation
novelty: First unified framework for comparing uncertainty-aware reward models across multiple dimensions, revealing design insights overlooked in prior work.
method_summary: Trains four UQ architectures using embeddings from pretrained LLMs, modifies standard losses for each method, and evaluates on standard metrics with a unified procedure.
method_components: MLP head ensembles with regularization terms
LoRA adapter ensembles for parameter efficiency
DPO-based Monte Carlo dropout for implicit reward uncertainty
Bayesian linear heads with Laplace approximation
key_innovation: Unified evaluation framework enabling systematic comparison of UQ methods across accuracy, calibration, and ranking dimensions.
assumptions: Epistemic uncertainty can be quantified via model ensembles/dropout/Bayesian methods
Embeddings from pretrained LLMs capture relevant features
Standard accuracy/calibration metrics adequately evaluate UQ performance
Model size/initialization dominate over architectural differences
theoretical_grounding: References Laplace approximation for BAY-LIN, standard RLHF theory, and uncertainty quantification principles; no new theoretical proofs provided.
title: RewardUQ: A Unified Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Reward Models
